interactive.rationale: Some controls present but limited or no script wiring — interactivity feels under-built.

references.rationale: Concept page has no chromestatus.com/feature link at all — fails CLAUDE.md rule #3.

design_system.rationale: Design-system audit: contains 4 raw hex colour token(s) instead of the design-system variables.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept's lede mirrors the feature index promise — same nouns, same scope.

spec_match.rationale: Concept demonstrates part of the feature but several keywords from the feature-index lede do not appear on the page — likely missing some surface.

edge_cases.rationale: Page only walks the happy path — there is no visible branch for failure, denial, or invalid input.

openQuestions.title: Wire the controls to live behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Controls exist but don't appear to drive a live readout. Hook each control to an event listener that re-renders the demo and shows the resulting state.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com self-heal link
openQuestions.detail: Add a 'see also' entry linking to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5093129273999360. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires every concept page to include this link so the fix-slugs.py recovery script can identify the folder if names get garbled.

openQuestions.title: Add a direct spec link
openQuestions.detail: References list only points back to the feature index or ChromeStatus. Add a direct link to the relevant spec (W3C draft, WICG explainer, or RFC) so readers can reach the normative source in one click.

openQuestions.title: Resolve a design-system finding
openQuestions.detail: The page contains 4 raw hex colour token(s) instead of the design-system variables. CLAUDE.md rules #5/#6/#7 require CSS variables, paired button background+color overrides, and zero static-card patterns.

openQuestions.title: Cover more of the spec surface
openQuestions.detail: The feature-level lede references API surface that this concept does not appear to demonstrate. Either expand this concept or carve a sibling concept that hits the missing capability.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently has no failure branch. Add a visible second branch that exercises an error path: unsupported browser, permission denied, malformed input, or quota-exceeded. The contrast is what teaches the spec.

summary: Needs work: 2 rubric items fail. Address the major open questions before counting this as a finished concept.
